reasoning: The two prominent peaks at 380 cm⁻¹ and 406 cm⁻¹ are characteristic of MoS2, corresponding to the E₂g¹ (in-plane) and A₁g (out-of-plane) vibrational modes, respectively. The frequency difference Δ ≈ 26 cm⁻¹ between these two modes is a well-established indicator of monolayer MoS2; in bulk MoS2 this separation is typically ~25–26 cm⁻¹ wider (Δ ≈ 25 for monolayer vs ~25+ for few-layer). The sharp peak widths (FWHM ~8 cm⁻¹) indicate high crystalline quality. The E₂g¹ mode at 380 cm⁻¹ is blue-shifted slightly compared to bulk (~382 cm⁻¹) and the A₁g at 406 cm⁻¹ is red-shifted compared to bulk (~408 cm⁻¹), both consistent with monolayer thickness. No additional peaks suggest contamination or mixed phases.
